meta.title: pan

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 3 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: pan
root.mentor_note: pan claims everything, and the words built on it rarely undersell that.

attempt.direction_prompt: A view of one corner, or the whole scene at once?

context_sentences: From the tower, the city offered a panorama that stretched all the way to the coast.
The brochure’s photo barely captured the panorama visible from the ridge at sunrise.
Within months, an outbreak confined to one province had grown into a global pandemic.
Historians still debate exactly how many lives the pandemic of 1918 claimed worldwide.
The temple’s carvings depicted the entire pantheon standing in a single unbroken row.
Critics now rank her among the pantheon of the country’s greatest living novelists.
The consultant’s new system was sold as a panacea, though it solved exactly one of the firm’s five problems.
No single reform will act as a panacea for a school system this unevenly funded.

construct_options.text: A remedy claimed to solve every problem at once
A medicine made from a single rare herb
A committee formed to review every complaint

parts.gloss: relating to a whole class or type

context_sentences: The biologist described the mutation as panorthic, present in every member of the isolated population.
A panorthic shift in migration timing had been recorded across the entire species, not just one flock.

construct_options.text: Affecting every member of an entire population or species
Occurring only in a single, unusually large individual
Passed down directly from parent to child
